items.title: Mystery surrounds suspected people smuggler in WA's far-north

children.content: Months after the arrival of six Chinese nationals in one of Australia's most isolated communities, questions remain about another man suspected of attempting to assist them.

gallery.altText: a graphic of people on a boat

thumbnail.altText: a graphic of people on a boat

items.title: WA considering separate stockpile of diesel amid fuel shortages

children.content: The WA government is considering creating its own "strategic stockpile" of diesel, saying the state's size and the reliance of the agriculture and resources industries on the fuel warrants a separate reserve.

image.altText: A hand on a diesel pump at a petrol station.

items.title: 'Missed opportunity': Astronomers lament loss of telescope partnership

children.content: The Artemis II mission used space technology developed in Australia, but experts fear the axing of a multimillion-dollar telescope partnership may stifle innovation and astronomy.

image.altText: Five buildings, four of which are firing lasers from their rooftops, against a starry Milky Way backdrop.

items.title: Emergency warning issued after blaze at Perth battery recycling facility

children.content: A man is taken to hospital and a HAZMAT emergency warning is issued after a serious fire breaks out at a lithium-ion battery recycling facility in Perth.

items.title: Calls for greater protections for Rottnest after Indigenous remains discovered

children.content: Aboriginal leaders call for greater protections for Rottnest Island after the discovery of Indigenous remains during construction work at the popular holiday resort last week.

image.altText: An aerial photo of the Bathurst Lighthouse in front of a series of premium tents at Rottnest Island.

items.title: An Aussie rugby win at home is one thing but victory in NZ is another

children.content: Australian teams will only boost their chances of achieving the ultimate success in Super Rugby Pacific if they conquer the challenge of beating their New Zealand opponents across the Tasman.

image.altText: Carter Gordon of the Reds holds the ball as he is tackled by the Crusaders.

items.title: Caravan park residents evicted after change of ownership

children.content: Reg Hoyling thought his park home in Albany was where he would spend the rest of his life, but a sale of the caravan park meant he was among the tenants evicted.

items.title: Firefighter encouraging more women to join front line

children.content: Beelerup firefighter Kahlia Lloyd says joining the brigade was the most empowering thing she had done.

gallery.altText: Woman looking at camera standing in front of fire truck.

thumbnail.altText: Woman looking at camera standing in front of fire truck.

items.title: Canada has flipped the script on child care. Could Australia copy?

children.content: Australia definitely won't nationalise child care, even though the data suggests there's a strong case. Perhaps a more feasible idea would be to copy what Canada has done.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: 'Like a flea on an elephant's back': The unexpected rise of solar power

children.content: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

images.altText: Rory McIlroy clenches his fist among fans at the Masters.

children.content: Rory McIlroy, hunting a second straight Masters victory, recovers from an early double bogey as three-time runner-up Justin Rose slips up after streaking to the top of the leaderboard in the final round at Augusta. Follow live.

items.title: Rory McIlroy makes his move as Justin Rose wobbles in Masters finale

updates.title: Clutch birdie putt from McIlroy takes him two clear as Rose three-putts
McIlroy locks in lead in Amen Corner

items.title: Fuel crisis has some some delaying retirement and working from home

children.content: The COVID-19 pandemic brought job losses, working from home and economic instability. Some fear the US-Israel war on Iran and spiking fuel prices have created similar struggles.

image.altText: A woman sitting with a guitar looking away from the camera.

items.title: Albanese and Wong to visit Brunei and Malaysia to shore up fuel supply

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese will travel to Brunei and Malaysia this week as part of efforts to shore up Australia's fuel and fertiliser supply. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands in front of an Australian flag.

items.title: Quiz: Jump in, we're going back in time

children.content: Which state had Australia's only communist MP? And where did the Titanic sink?

image.altText: The RMS Titanic leaves Belfast for sea trials.

items.title: Markets on edge, oil set to rise on US Gulf blockade threats

children.content: The Middle East failed peace talks are expected to drive oil prices higher and reverse the anticipated gains on the ASX today. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: 3D printed oil barrels and rising stock graph

items.title: Major setback for beekeepers as varroa mite chemical resistance detected again

children.content: There are growing fears that a second varroa mite strain could enter Australia, as beekeepers lose key chemical options for controlling the tiny parasites of honey bees.

gallery.altText: A varroa mite on a honey bee

thumbnail.altText: A varroa mite on a honey bee

items.title: Heroes of Bondi stabbing awarded for their bravery two years on

children.content: Two years on from the Westfield Bondi Junction stabbing attack, eight heroes have been recognised in a special Australian Bravery Decorations honours list.

image.altText: Muhammad Taha sitting on a grey couch wearing a black hoodie.

items.title: Four dead after fatal crashes on WA roads

children.content: Four people have died on WA roads after two separate crashes in the space of just over 15 hours.

items.title: 'They're not just big fluffy puppy dogs': Breeder warning over Highland cattle popularity

children.content: Registered Highland cattle breeders have more than tripled in Western Australia in the past five years. The shaggy Scottish breed is gaining popularity across WA, with more breeders interested in taking on the cattle and strong demand from agritourism.

items.title: How to make your workplace dog-friendly

children.content: How can employers design dog-friendly workplaces if they want to, and what does the law say about animals at work?

items.title: 'He didn't want to speak to me': Port star speaks after report over language

children.content: Zak Butters defends himself after the Power star was reported for using inappropriate language towards an umpire.

items.title: Plans to move residents off Cocos Islands walked back after backlash

children.content: After seething criticism from residents, the Commonwealth has released its final plan to protect the Cocos (Keeling) Islands from rising sea levels.

image.altText: White surf crashes against a seawall of stacked sand bags, with tropical plants and coconut trees in the background.

items.title: UK man jailed for abusing girl may face renewed visa cancellation

children.content: A British child sex offender who avoided deportation is facing a possible visa review, after an ABC investigation.

image.altText: A swing at an Adelaide playground site.

items.title: Ban on live sheep trade has another knock-on effect on WA wool

children.content: Elders is relocating its Western Australian wool handling and marketing operations to Victoria in response to declining wool production volumes in WA.
